executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict has reached a critical inflection point with confirmed US combat casualties (3 killed, 5 wounded) and Iranian retaliation striking Gulf bases. Supreme Leader Khamenei's death in the Feb 28 US-Israeli strike has triggered massive Iranian retaliation across the region, including attacks on Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar, Erbil, and direct missile strikes on Israeli civilian areas. Over 1,000 US strikes on Iranian targets confirmed. The UAE has closed its Tehran embassy and the UK is planning mass evacuations as the conflict expands beyond a purely military confrontation.
whats_new: US Central Command confirms 3 US service members killed and 5 seriously wounded in Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks on Gulf bases - first American combat deaths
IRGC claims complete destruction of Ali Al-Salem naval base in Kuwait and 4 drone strikes on Salman Port in Bahrain, plus 3 US/UK oil tankers struck in Persian Gulf
Iranian ballistic missile strike on Beit Shemesh, Israel kills 9 civilians with 11 missing - highest single-day Israeli casualty toll in conflict
UAE announces closure of Tehran embassy and withdrawal of all diplomatic staff - major diplomatic rupture between Iran and Gulf state
UK Foreign Office begins planning evacuation of tens of thousands of British citizens from the Middle East
Texas shooting leaves 2 dead, 14 injured - FBI treating as potential Iran-linked terrorism, shooter wore Iranian flag shirt
French nuclear carrier Charles de Gaulle changes course, deployment shortened amid ongoing hostilities
confirmed_events: CENTCOM confirms 3 US soldiers killed and 5 wounded in Iranian retaliatory strikes on Gulf bases - marking direct US casualties in escalating conflict
US-Israeli airstrike killed Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei on February 28 along with senior officials - confirmed by multiple sources
Death toll rises to 153 female students in US-Israeli strike on Minab girls' school in Hormozgan province - Iranian Education Ministry confirmed
IRGC conducting widespread strikes across Gulf: Kuwait (Ali Al-Salem base), Bahrain (Salman Port), Qatar (Al-Udeid), Erbil (US base), and Persian Gulf tanker attacks
Israeli Defense Forces report 2,000 bombs dropped on Iran since conflict began - equivalent to 50% of total from June 2025's 12 Day War
UAE closes Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange and Dubai Financial Market March 2-3 following Iranian missile strikes on Gulf territory
unverified_emerging: Unconfirmed reports former Iranian president Mahmoud Ahmadinejad killed in strike on Tehran home - source: state media claims requiring verification
Unconfirmed explosion reported in Tehran during ongoing US-Iran conflict - potential major escalation signal
IRGC claims strike on USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier - Pentagon denies report, conflicting claims on maritime engagement
Turkish border sources report Iranians trapped at Khoy border gate attempting to flee - unverified civilian displacement claim

people_travel: UK Foreign Office actively planning evacuation of tens of thousands of British citizens from the Middle East - significant displacement operation initiated
UAE capital markets closed March 2-3 as regional financial hubs halt operations following Iran strikes on Gulf targets
Travel advisory escalation expected across Gulf states as Iranian proxy threats expand beyond military targets
supply_chain: Strait of Hormuz under direct threat - Iran has repeatedly threatened closure and struck tankers, global oil supply routes at risk
UAE ports and financial markets closed March 2-3, disrupting regional shipping and trade flows
Three US and British oil tankers struck in Persian Gulf by IRGC - significant impact on energy shipping lanes
market_macro: UAE stock markets closed for two consecutive days - first such emergency closure in recent memory
Global energy markets on edge as Iran war threatens oil supplies through Strait of Hormuz
Regional airline stocks and tourism sector facing severe disruption as conflict expands beyond military zones

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict is likely to intensify over the next 24-72 hours as Iran responds to the decapitation strike on Khamenei with continued retaliation across the Gulf and against Israel. US force posture indicates sustained operations with over 1,000 targets struck and more aircraft carriers converging on the region. Iranian proxy attacks (Hezbollah, Houthi) will continue expanding the front beyond the Gulf. Civilian casualties on all sides are expected to rise significantly.
escalation_risks: Iranian threats to close Strait of Hormuz becoming more credible as tanker attacks increase - could trigger global energy crisis
Hezbollah expanding rocket/drone attacks on Israel from Lebanon - northern Israel front widening
Possible Iranian sleeper cell activation in US or allied territory - Texas shooting may be early signal
Russian or Chinese intervention could escalate conflict beyond regional bounds
Turkey border instability as Iranian citizens attempt mass flight from conflict zones
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: Diplomatic channels remain theoretically open despite battlefield escalation - Trump previously acknowledged 'new leadership' talks, though Khamenei's death complicates any negotiation with Iran's current leadership. However, battlefield momentum and civilian casualty images (Minab school, Beit Shemesh) create powerful incentives for continued fighting rather than compromise. Any de-escalation would require major third-party mediation (Turkey, Oman, or UN) and face significant domestic political opposition on all sides.
